turns.user_message: Someone made a passing comment yesterday about how I always seem to second-guess myself, and it just stuck in my head all day. I keep replaying the moment over and over, wondering if they actually see something I have been ignoring. It feels like every little choice I make is suddenly under a microscope, and I am completely exhausted by it. I wish I could just brush it off like a normal person would, but instead I am letting it eat away at my confidence. It is so frustrating to feel this shaken up over just a few casual words.

That same feeling has been creeping into everything I do over the past few days, turning simple tasks into heavy burdens. I catch myself pausing before I speak or act, terrified that I am going to prove them right. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own behavior, looking for flaws that probably are not even there. Every time I try to push through the doubt, it just comes rushing back twice as strong. I feel like I am slowly losing my footing, and I do not know how to stop it.

Now I am completely convinced that I am fundamentally flawed, and it feels like there is no escaping this heavy realization. I look at every interaction and only see proof that I am not enough, no matter how hard I try to pretend otherwise. The doubt has taken over my entire headspace, making it impossible to trust my own instincts or make a single decision without spiraling. I just feel so hollowed out by all this self-criticism, like I am carrying a weight that will never lift. I hate that I let a few careless words completely dismantle how I see myself.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This just started recently.
